The M3D approach generates strong adversarial examples to attack two substitute models, aiming to move the adversarial examples closer to the target class, thus making them more robust to variations in the black-box model.
The M3D approach involves a training pipeline consisting of a generative model G and two discriminators D1 and D2.
The clean images pass through the generative model G to get their adversarial versions, which are then fed into the discriminators D1 and D2 during training.

Our proposed approach outperforms all existing methods for black-box targeted attacks with a clear margin
In the all-source setting, our method outperforms the recent state-of-the-art TTP for black-box targeted attack by 12.75% to 41.23%
When the substitute models are VGG19BN, DenseNet121, ResNet50, our method averagely outperforms TTP by 36.69%, 31.56%, 13.98% respectively
Our method shows impressive improvements when the black-box model has a large model discrepancy from the substitute model
For example, when the substitute model is VGG19BN and the black-box model is ResNet152, our method can reach 68.41% target accuracy while the TTP method is 27.18%
These results clearly demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods for black-box targeted attacks
